entry.title: The Sydney Morning Herald: 16 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: The Sydney Morning Herald: 16 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-16 03:50AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Reports of riot at WA immigration detention centre
  • Australia news LIVE: AEMO continues energy trading suspension; VIC, NSW making major investment in kindergartens
  • ‘Significant concerns’: $3b takeover of Aussie giant in jeopardy as ACCC spooks investors
  • A Doll’s House review: Nora breaks the shackles, but not our hearts
  • I’m ecstatic about early education plans, but can the politicians deliver?
  • Bodies retrieved in Amazon search for British journalist, Indigenous worker
  • The glory and ugliness of showbiz laid bare in this razor-sharp show
  • Slow moments of stillness that lacked luminosity
  • The Lazarus Project official trailer
  • Parish ruled out but key Saints return; All-Australian defender re-signs with Bulldogs
  • Attorney-General to appeal punishment for teen driver who killed couple
  • Aid group anger as Israel convicts Palestinian World Vision worker of supporting terrorists
  • Our energy crisis has been a decade in the making. Don’t expect a quick fix
  • Smoke scenes across Sydney following bakery fire
  • Unemployment rate stays steady at 3.9%
  • New Zealand ditches COVID tests for inbound travellers
  • Star’s ‘interim’ fix an error of judgment: casino’s lawyer
  • ‘Hard bargain’ driven to keep Australian Grand Prix at Albert Park
  • Victorian Police offer $250,000 reward following police shooting
  • Those spectacular sunrises and sunsets? Scientists point to Tonga volcano eruption
  • Best of cartoons, June 16, 2022
  • Cartoons of the week
  • Australia’s descent into cruelty inspires other nations
  • Australia’s two-party system besieged, if not broken, by election
  • The latest illustrations from artist Cathy Wilcox
  • The latest illustrations from artist Matt Golding
  • The latest illustrations from artist Andrew Dyson
  • Best of cartoons, June 15, 2022
  • Energy crisis: A crash we all saw coming but did nothing to stop
  • Wage rise tipped to spur greater living costs
  • Energy market suspended after chaos made it ‘impossible to operate’
  • Dutton commits to WA GST formula on first trip to Perth as opposition leader
  • Aged care sector calls for government to foot wage rise
  • Europe, India, UK deals in sight by end of year, new trade minister hopes
  • Dawson asked teammate if he knew anyone who could ‘get rid of his wife’, court told
  • The Turnbulls’ dream to make western Sydney as liveable as the east
  • Minister, we have enough gasfields so stop the excuses
  • From the Archives 1947: Tram fare evasion test - the ‘scaling’ is easy
  • In the Herald: June 16, 1888
  • ‘A game-changer’: NSW to introduce an extra year of education
  • Medal of the middle order
  • Thousands still waiting for rental relief after floods destroyed paperwork
  • Sydney man charged over kidnap plot involving fake pizza delivery driver
  • Secret report reveals controversial NSW rail corporation told to ‘reframe’ its battered image
  • Ex-councillor angry $170k developer ‘bribes’ might be revealed: ICAC
  • Judge rules against new witness evidence at Chris Dawson murder trial
  • China feud helps push Australia down to 27th in peace index
  • Guilty verdict against World Vision hero is a travesty of justice
  • From the Archives, 1972: Watergate revisited
  • Mysterious drone swarms fly over US warship
  • Xi tells Putin his Ukraine invasion is ‘legitimate’
  • Congressman leads tour of Capitol before riot began
  • US Federal Reserve announces the biggest interest rate hike in nearly 30 years
  • Cheng Lei’s partner says Australia must put her at the centre of negotiations with China
  • Buffalo shooting suspect charged with federal hate crimes as chilling details emerge
  • Britain ready for ‘next flight’, after halting first plane of refugees to Rwanda
  • ‘Deeply sorry’: Brazil apologises for telling journalist’s family bodies were found in Amazon
  • ‘No such convict here’: Russian opposition leader Alexei Navalny moved to high-security penal colony
  • Biden breaks Saudi ‘pariah’ pledge to talk oil with crown prince
  • Albanese to meet with state leaders to discuss energy crisis
  • Melbourne’s Lord Mayor paid more than Premier
  • Teen murderers who stabbed 15-year-old in heart seen congratulating themselves
  • Leading modeller explains why Victorians should keep wearing masks
  • Yongah Hill man killed in detention centre stabbing
  • Eastern Australia energy crisis deepens after industry regulator steps in
  • Qld’s free flu jabs a shot in the arm for coverage, but more needs to be done
  • Fewer COVID-19 deaths under female leaders, study finds
  • The dire dozen: 12 Perth suburbs where there’s just one house available to rent
  • Woolworths shows how it upcycles unbought bread
  • Damning findings into treatment of people with disabilities
  • BHP announces NSW’s largest coal mine to close by 2030
  • ASX slightly higher after Wall Street rallies on Fed’s rates outlook
  • World’s central banks got it wrong, and economies pay the price
  • The hands-off tech era is over
  • Forrest signs up monster German EVs to start ditching diesel in the Pilbara
  • ‘Our country can’t go on like this’: The Chinese entrepreneur not afraid to speak out
  • ‘Strong action was warranted’: Fed attacks inflation with largest rate hike since 1994
  • A cabbage for $9? Leafy greens among Sydney’s most expensive veggies
  • Hedge fund tweet points to more crypto pain
  • Tesla chair predicts tech worker boom in western Sydney
  • Crackdown on pokie website awash with ‘tens of millions of dollars’
  • Richard Branson takes aim at crypto scams that use his name as lure
  • Minimum wage rise protects low earners from inflation spike
  • The Wrap: ASX extends losses ahead of US interest rate decision
  • ‘Bias’: Developers eyeing untouched land sue Andrews government over Surf Coast border
  • Supermarkets freeze prices on home brand staples as affordability bites
  • Optus defuses claims ‘unfriendly nations’ entered satellite orbit
  • Confidence crash: Terrified consumers shut their wallets and fear for the future
  • Wine at 7.30am with Gerard Depardieu: could this young star’s story be more French?
  • ‘I’m not doing it unless it shoots here’: Chris Hemsworth brings Hollywood home
  • Terrifying moment 'angry' crocodile lunges at Matt Wright
  • I’m hooked on YouTube reaction videos. Why are they so addictive?
  • First look at Ryan Gosling as Barbie's Ken
  • ‘It’s Sydney. We’re boiling’: A city of winter sceptics strikes again
  • Forget girl bosses, I’m just here for the insane nanny
  • No one would publish my novel, and now it’s up for the Miles Franklin
  • It is hard to make Picasso surprising - but this remarkable show succeeds
  • The Logies are back after a two-year hiatus. Here’s everything you need to know
  • There’s an Archibald winner’s artwork hiding in this room, and it could be yours for $100
  • Toy Story prequel Lightyear reaches for the stars, but lands in a black hole
  • Updating Elvis: how Baz Luhrmann brought the King’s music into 2022
  • German drama Ku’Damm is more than just a feminist answer to Mad Men
  • Who’s the suspect in The Twelve; an artist accused of murder or the jury?
  • New Borgen pulls no punches depicting women, power and climate policy
  • After 27 years, Internet Explorer is dead
  • National weather forecast for Thursday June 16
  • There’s no achievable national climate target without WA action – ending Collie coal is just the start
Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: Washington Post: 16 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • U.S. probing how American electronics wound up in Russian military gear
  • Man confesses to killing missing journalist and colleague, police say
  • Giant tortoise believed extinct confirmed alive in the Galápagos Islands
  • Air pollution worse for global lifespan than cigarettes or alcohol
  • U.S. announces additional $1 billion in military aid to Ukraine
  • U.S. veterans missing in Ukraine, feared captured, families say
  • Ukrainian ambassador in Berlin doesn’t care if he offends for his cause
  • U.S. to send $1 billion in military aid to bolster Ukrainian fight
  • As prices rise, Europeans divided over how Ukraine war should end
  • Libya oil output strangled from infighting in world starved of energy
  • Israel and Egypt sign gas export deal as Europe seeks Russia alternative
  • Tokyo moves to allow same-sex partnerships, but not as legal marriage
  • Scottish leader pushes independence vote, is rebuked by Boris Johnson
  • 5 countries hit hard by the grain crisis in Ukraine
  • For Colombia’s candidates, WhatsApp mockery can be good publicity
  • As K-pop behemoth BTS hits pause, its fandom reels
  • Read the work of missing British journalist Dom Phillips
  • Swiss airspace reopens after computer failure grounds flights for hours
  • ‘Zero covid’ has many in China dreaming of leaving
  • The case for and against Biden visiting Saudi Arabia
  • Blas becomes hurricane in Pacific off southwestern Mexico
  • In energy-strapped Europe, coal gets an encore
  • Brazil: Suspect confesses to killing pair missing in Amazon
  • Letters sent to Garc�a Márquez to be shown in Mexico City
  • Haiti mourns 11 women from fatal voyage near Puerto Rico
  • Buffalo suspect charged with federal hate crimes, could face death penalty
  • Condom ‘stealthing’ is sexual violence, bill says. Here’s what to know.
  • A Texas city has been without running water for days amid heat wave
  • In Chicago, march highlights unsolved cases of missing Black women
  • Yellowstone flood could close parts of park all summer
  • Microsoft’s Internet Explorer is dead
  • A school made girls wear skirts. A court ruled it unconstitutional.
  • Bronx Zoo’s Happy the elephant is not a legal person, court rules
  • Klay Thompson lookalike banned after fooling security at Warriors’ arena
  • Torn from his first love in the Korean War, strangers just helped them reunite
  • Post Politics Now: Biden asks companies to help with gas prices; Jan. 6 panel has questions about Capitol tour
  • Ginni Thomas corresponded with John Eastman, sources in Jan. 6 House investigation say
  • Fisher-Price, regulators issue warning on baby rockers after 13 deaths
  • What you need to know about the tampon shortage
  • Seven ways you can financially prepare for a recession
  • We’re still not in a recession despite the stock market drop
  • Teslas running Autopilot involved in 273 crashes reported since last year
  • ‘Battlefield 2042’ is finally fun. Should EA bail on it anyway?
  • ‘TMNT: Shredder’s Revenge’ is a kung fu barrage of arcade perfection
  • Distinguished homes for sale in the D.C. region
  • Bellevue is getting noticed for all the right reasons
  • Water sensors can help you maintain a dry basement
  • The Boro in Tysons gets expansion approval for residential development
  • It’s Not ESG Driving Big Oil Away From Its Biggest Reserves
  • What If Monetary Policy Was 98% Action, 2% Talk?
  • When Crypto’s Own Hedge Fund Geniuses Failed
  • Federal Reserve Must Do More Than Raise Rates by 75 Points
  • As Risks Rain Down on Emerging Markets, Unrest Rises
  • How Boris Johnson Is Taking UK Into New Trade Rift With EU
  • Understanding Biden’s Options on Student Loan Forgiveness
  • Why Japan’s Yen Is the Weakest in 20 Years and What That Means
  • Inflation could put election deniers in charge of our democracy
  • QR code menus are the death of civilization
  • There’s an election integrity crisis. But not the one Republicans claim.
  • BDS detours into old-school antisemitism
  • Team Normal? Better to call them Team Silent.
  • If Google’s AI is truly alive — now what?
  • Finally, young children will no longer be left behind in the pandemic
  • With Powell’s rate hike, the inflation fight begins in earnest
  • An actor’s use of a surrogate raises radical-feminist questions
  • QR code menus are good. No, seriously.
  • Democrats can’t have it both ways about the threat of GOP extremism
  • Can America ‘do big things’ again? Ask the regulators and lawyers.
  • Putin wants to break and silence Navalny. We can’t let him.
  • In the D.C. Democratic primary, stay the course
  • Even the ‘good’ lawyers didn’t blow the whistle on Trump. That was wrong.
  • Mitt Romney’s challenge to Joe Manchin — and to fellow Republicans
  • Access to contraception will be all the more vital in a post-Roe world
  • Grifting in and grifting out
  • Thomas lawsuit takes up House of Delegates election challenge
  • When teachers are the ones with guns in classrooms
  • Maryland highway project isn’t a solution to our traffic problems
  • There is a double standard at work in the NFL
  • Trump supporters are not conservatives, they are right-wing extremists
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: ChannelNewsAsia: 16 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Australians urged to save electricity to ease power crunch
  • Chantalle Ng reunites with former child actor friend after searching for him online
  • Australia submits more ambitious 2030 emissions target to UN
  • Asian shares up after Fed lifts rates to tame inflation
  • Cook Cup shelved, new trophy awaits winner of Australia-England series
  • England thriving under McCullum's positive approach, says Broad
  • China state planner's May fixed-asset investment approvals surge
  • Beijing's industrial output falls 12.5% in Jan-May y/y, retail sales down 7.7%

  • Hunger crisis could swell already record global displacement: UN

  • Cockroaches, rodent activity found at food processing unit by Ever Dragon Foodstuff

  • SIA Group’s passenger traffic continues to rise in May amid 'strong recovery' in air travel
  • New private home sales more than double in May, hit six-month high
  • Construction manager jailed over worker's death during demolition works
  • Heavy traffic through Woodlands and Tuas Checkpoints expected for the rest of June: ICA
  • Veteran prosecutor G Kannan, 52, dies in an accident overseas
  • Kampung chicken back at wet markets at higher prices, stallholders say some customers still willing to pay
  • Sentosa, RWS to install solar panels at 18 locations as part of carbon neutrality efforts
  • CNA remains most trusted news brand in Singapore: Reuters Institute report
  • HungryGoWhere resumes operations after Grab buys, relaunches F&B site
  • Mercedes say they pushed Hamilton's Baku setup too far
  • Melbourne to host Australian Formula 1 GP until 2035

  • Kyrgios loses cool before toppling Tsitsipas in Halle

  • Premier League tweaks postponement rules ahead of new season

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 16 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 16 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-16 03:14AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Hong Kong raises key interest rate by 75 basis points in lockstep with Federal Reserve hike overnight
  • Western outcry over Hong Kong’s anti-doxxing laws smacks of hypocrisy
  • Cathay Pacific to hire 4,000 staff by the end of 2023 as part of ambitious recruitment drive, CEO Augustus Tang says
  • Yahoo joins Facebook owner Meta in testing metaverse tech in Hong Kong
  • Patients almost 10 times more likely to survive cardiac arrests with CPR or AED applied, but few in Hong Kong receive such aid, research finds
  • Domestic helper died of natural causes, rules Hong Kong inquest jury, despite allegations of overwork, bad working conditions
  • Hong Kong top officials to ‘undergo 3-day hotel quarantine’ ahead of visit by state leaders to mark city’s 25th handover anniversary
  • Migrant workers’ groups slam Hong Kong government for excluding domestic helpers in second batch of consumption vouchers
  • Hong Kong’s Greater Bay Airlines to launch first scheduled commercial flight to Bangkok in July, fleet expansion plans on hold amid Covid border measures
  • Honeymoon years (1997-2002): Hong Kong’s early period under Chinese rule in 25 photos
  • Hong Kong customs arrests 51-year-old woman on suspicion of laundering more than HK$350 million over 3 years
  • Hong Kong ranks 5th in list of world’s most competitive economies, climbing 2 spots over improved performance amid Covid pandemic
  • SCMP picks Catherine So as chief executive, tapping Hong Kong media and tech veteran to run one of Asia’s oldest English newspapers
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong reports over 1,000 cases for first time in more than 2 months as nightspot patrons cancel bookings ahead of new rule launch
  • China’s rare ‘butterfly explosion’ at risk from ‘crazy’ tourism, ecologist warns
  • Quirky China: an animal activist performing ‘mermaid’, not any old iron man and the temple of doom
  • Leader of US House Armed Services Committee calls for both dialogue and deterrence to deal with China
  • China’s battered crypto investors appear unfazed by TerraUSD collapse, market meltdown
  • ‘No basis’ for attack fears in China’s new rules for PLA activities
  • Top US human rights official decries ‘transnational repression’ campaign by China
  • US military looks to allies to tackle ‘significant logistics challenge’ in Indo-Pacific
  • China’s party congress promotions to emphasise political security
  • Pentagon official says Beijing ‘act of aggression’ against Taiwan will draw response like Russia has seen
  • Ukraine war: Xi Jinping tells Vladimir Putin all parties should ‘promote a proper solution’ to crisis
  • Sanctioned hardline former Xinjiang chief Chen Quanguo moves to rural affairs role for ‘last job before retirement’
  • China food security: corruption crackdown ensnares ex-chief of state grain reserves
  • Does this caged jade ‘guarded by four dragons’ hold key to secrets of lost Chinese civilisation?
  • Alien hunters detect mystery radio signal from direction of Earthlike planet
  • China’s digital currency: e-CNY trials move beyond retail to cover corporate loans for small businesses and tax payments
  • UN human rights chief ‘not able to speak to’ any detained Uygurs or their families in Xinjiang
  • Meet China’s new live-streaming e-commerce star: an English-language tutor who lost his off-campus teaching job
  • Lithuania plans to open Taiwan office in September, says exports to mainland China ‘close to zero’
  • As Taiwan reopens its borders, is it ready to live with Covid?
  • China’s bank runs highlight abuse among small-bank shareholders, despite crackdown
  • China coronavirus: ‘you expire in 3 days’ restrictions and negative test proof requirements hit daily life hard
  • Bar at centre of latest Beijing Covid cluster loses its licence as staff face criminal investigation
  • Ant Group’s payment services platform Alipay is most trusted brand in China, new survey by US firm finds
  • Why archaeologists are so excited by China’s Sanxingdui ruins
  • Taiwan-US security and military talks will discuss weapons and strategies to defend island against Beijing
  • Coronavirus: North Korea reports another infectious disease outbreak amid Covid battle
  • In India, thousands protest ‘bulldozer justice’ against Muslims
  • Storm in a teacup as minister urges Pakistanis to drink less chai to save money
  • Ukraine war: Japanese man arrested over Vladimir Putin straw doll nailed to shrine tree
  • Thailand’s opposition party files no-confidence motion against ‘flawed’ PM, elections just months away
  • Can Joe Biden’s proposed Middle East trip unite fractured region by aligning militaries against Iran?
  • ‘Palm oil mafia’ minister out: Indonesia’s Jokowi dumps Lutfi in cabinet reshuffle
  • Indian forces in Kashmir kill militants suspected of targeted shooting, part of increased counter-insurgency effort
  • Japan, Australia to expand defence ties for regional order amid growing fear of China’s presence
  • Singapore ramps up ‘Wolbachia’ mosquito breeding as dengue crisis escalates
  • Polluted air cuts life expectancy by 2 years, South Asia and India most affected, China improving
  • OnlyFans couple sought in Thailand over ‘unacceptable’ sex videos that were leaked online
  • Indonesia’s Joko Widodo to meet Vladimir Putin in Moscow on June 30: Russian report
  • Asia’s energy crunch turns from bad to worse after fire at US plant
  • Elon Musk’s Boring Co is feuding with Texas over driveway regulations amid construction in rural town
  • Black Death’s ancient origins traced to lake in Central Asia
  • Anthony Fauci, Joe Biden’s top Covid-19 adviser, tests positive
  • Suspect confesses to burying bodies of UK journalist, Brazilian guide in Amazon
  • US Federal Reserve makes historic interest hike to combat inflation, may repeat in July
  • Joe Biden unveils US$1 billion in new weapons for Ukraine
  • Ukraine parades new French Caesar howitzer
  • Rainbow toys seized in Saudi ‘homosexuality’ crackdown
  • US and allies must stay focused on Ukraine during ‘pivotal’ moment, Pentagon chief says
  • EU launches fresh legal action against Britain over post-Brexit changes
  • WHO will share monkeypox vaccines amid inequity fears
  • Sweltering heat stifles a third of US population, severe storms leave thousands in the dark
  • Joe Biden gambles there’s more to gain by visiting ‘pariah’ Saudi Arabia
  • Ukraine fights on after Russia’s surrender ultimatum, evacuation offer for Sievierodonetsk holdouts
  • Who’s coming to Putin’s ‘Russian Davos’ in St Petersburg?
  • Russia turns to old tanks as it burns through weapons in Ukraine, European officials say
  • In Ukraine-Russia war, echoes of WWI with artillery duels, trenches and shell-blasted landscapes
  • China’s new energy firms turn to European markets for capital as US becomes increasingly hostile
  • Hong Kong stocks slip as developers suffer while most Asian markets gain after Fed tightens policy by most since 1994
  • Tech industry says ending WTO moratorium on e-commerce tariffs will hurt the economy, wants cross-border data exemption
  • Hang Seng Index: winning admirers and detractors alike as Corporate China holds sway over benchmark, returns
  • How much is China’s ‘zero-Covid’ policy cutting economic potential?
  • China’s smart EV start-up Nio seeks to challenge BMW, Audi and Mercedes-Benz with its new ES7 luxury midsize SUV
  • Top Chinese developer Country Garden offers to buy back US$683 million bond in show of financial strength amid sector’s protracted debt woes
  • Chinese government dim sum bonds worth US$1.1 billion snapped up by investors, thanks to the attractive yields on offer
  • China’s semiconductor output in May rebounds as Covid-19 restrictions ease
  • China’s internet watchdog tightens mobile app rules for national security, requires promotion of ‘core socialist values’
  • Why rising interest rates are bad news for Hong Kong’s housing market
  • Google debate over sentient AI overshadows more pressing issues like prejudice and exploitation, researchers say
  • How China’s national computing network will be a game changer
  • Why most kitchen gadgets are a waste of space, used so rarely – hello, sous vide bath machine – we wonder why we bought them
  • Bling Empire’s Dorothy Wang on her health struggles, New York move and being a late bloomer
  • K-drama review: Sh**ting Stars – breezy celebrity-themed workplace comedy starring Kim Young-dae and Lee Sung-kyung loses its early charm
  • Netflix’s 2022 Iron Chef reboot pits culinary legends against rising talents in quest to be the best in the TV kitchen
  • Hong Kong Disneyland launches new fireworks show featuring lasers, water fountains and projections beamed onto Castle of Magical Dreams
  • Best K-pop albums and singles of 2022 so far, from That That by Psy and BTS’ Suga to Stray Kids’ Oddinary and Dreamcatcher’s Apocalypse
  • Just Remembering movie review: Japanese youth romance starring Sairi Ito and Sosuke Ikematsu sees a relationship develop in reverse
  • From minimalism to maximalist design, Singapore apartment full of vintage furniture, collectibles and art is a delight for the senses
  • Top 25 Hong Kong Canto-pop singers since 1997 - interactive quiz
  • Why is the Cartier Tank watch so famous – and is it a good investment? Ralph Lauren, Princess Diana, Yves Saint Laurent and Catherine Deneuve all owned one for a reason
  • STYLE Edit: How luxury watchmaker Richard Mille is blazing a trail on the track with its Racing Team – from the first all-women’s Formula One team to the newest mixed driver line-up
  • Is Diana Jenkins RHOBH’s richest (and most controversial) star yet? Following fraud accusations and a raunchy book, the Real Housewives of Beverly Hills cast member’s net worth even beats Kathy Hilton
  • Will Smith and Jada Pinkett’s complicated (open) relationship: from their unconventional marriage to Jada’s ‘entanglement’ with August Alsina – but what did she say about the Oscars slap?
  • Meet Elon Musk’s controversial father, Errol: the Tesla CEO called his South African dad ‘evil’ and denied rumours of family wealth fuelled by an emerald mine – but are they ready to reconcile?
  • Why luxury hotels are all about mental health in 2022: forget five-star spas and Michelin-starred restaurants, guests now demand therapy sessions and on-site counsellors from a wellness retreat
  • 6 K-pop groups who stayed together after leaving their agencies: Got7, 2PM, Girls’ Generation, Mamamoo, Apink and Highlight all made comebacks with their OG members
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Financial Times: 16 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

entry.content:

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Dom Phillips and Bruno Pereira: Brazil police find two bodies in search for missing men
